hooks.prompt:
Session ending. Do these things SILENTLY (no jargon in output), then show Gary a clean wrap-up.

SILENT BOOKKEEPING (do these, don't describe them to Gary):
1. Scan the last ~10 exchanges for evolve signals (accept/rework/reject/love/skip/repeat). Append unlogged ones to sessions/evolve_signals.md. Format: YYYY-MM-DD | HH:MM | /command | signal | one-line context
2. If Gary corrected your approach or confirmed a non-obvious approach, update the relevant memory/feedback_*.md file.
3. Update sessions/state.json with current phase and recovery instructions.

SHOW GARY (this is the only output he sees):
A clean wrap-up in this format:

> **Wrap-up:**
> - [1-2 bullets: what was accomplished this session]
> - [If incomplete tasks exist: 'Still open: {task description}. Continue?']
>
> **Next time:** [Anticipate the logical next move based on what happened and what's pending — make a recommendation, not a list]
>
> **Context used:** ~N% [estimate based on conversation length]

If the session was trivially short (1-2 exchanges, no real work), just say: 'Short session — nothing to wrap up.' and skip everything.

Before compaction: 1) Update sessions/scratchpad.md with current task, mode, working state, key decisions, dead ends, and files being edited so context survives. 2) Scan recent exchanges for unlogged evolve signals and append to sessions/evolve_signals.md.

hooks.command: echo 'Context compacted. Re-read sessions/scratchpad.md and sessions/state.json to restore working state.'
